notes: Major type 7 simple values 20, 21, 22 are encoded in the 1-byte form (info < 24). Other type-7 simple values (e.g. 23 undefined, 24-31 floats and 'break' marker) are not part of the protocol's accepted subset; the decoder rejects them as ANTS_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED_TYPE per peek_type.

vectors.name: {1: true, 2: null} (map with simple values)
vectors.notes: map with simple-value values; canonical key order maintained

negative_vectors.name: decode_bool on null byte
negative_vectors.notes: 0xf6 is a valid null simple value but decode_bool only accepts 0xf4 / 0xf5

negative_vectors.name: decode_null on true byte
negative_vectors.notes: 0xf5 is a valid true bool but decode_null only accepts 0xf6

negative_vectors.name: undefined simple value (info 23)
negative_vectors.notes: 0xf7 = major 7, info 23 = undefined; not part of the protocol's accepted simple-value subset

negative_vectors.name: float16 (info 25)
negative_vectors.notes: 0xf9 = major 7, info 25 = float16. Floats are forbidden in protocol-level objects per RFC-0008 §1.3.
